summary: Open sql command's SQLite connection in read-only mode so CTE-wrapped writes are rejected at the driver layer.
reason: Greptile P1 (security) flagged that the keyword-prefix DML blocklist could be bypassed with `WITH x AS (INSERT INTO ...) SELECT 1` — the head extracted from such a query is 'WITH X AS', which matches none of the banned prefixes. Because the `sql` command is annotated mcp:read-only, an MCP agent advertising this tool as non-destructive could be tricked into mutating the local store. Fix: open the connection via store.OpenReadOnly() which uses mode=ro at the driver, so writes are rejected regardless of how the SQL is shaped. The keyword prefix gate is kept only as a friendlier error for the common direct cases.
validated_outcome: Behavior tests: 'SELECT name FROM sqlite_master' succeeds; 'DROP TABLE x' rejected with the friendly DML error; 'WITH x AS (INSERT INTO watchlists(name) VALUES(...)) SELECT 1' rejected by the read-only driver.
